author: Dr. Lizard
title: What is the key flexibility difference between WHO-5 and ICC for genetically-defined MDS entities?
answer: WHO-5 allows diagnosis of genetic entities without dysplasia; ICC requires dysplasia except for multi-hit TP53.
detail: WHO-5 permits MDS-SF3B1 and MDS-biTP53 diagnosis based solely on genetic findings without morphologic dysplasia.
ICC generally requires 10% dysplasia threshold but makes exception for multi-hit TP53 cases.
This reflects WHO-5's greater emphasis on molecular genetics over traditional morphologic criteria.
The flexibility helps capture early or subtle disease that might be missed by morphology alone.
releaseNote: WHO-5 and ICC have different approaches to diagnosing genetically-defined MDS entities, with WHO-5 allowing diagnosis without dysplasia and ICC requiring dysplasia except for multi-hit TP53 cases.
